title: Lerchia baccata
taxonomy: Lerchia baccata is classified as a species within the genus Lerchia and family Annonaceae. The family Annonaceae is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the custard apple family. Lerchia baccata is native to tropical Africa, and its classification is part of the broader Angiosperm Phylogeny Group (APG) system. The APG system is a widely accepted classification of flowering plants that groups them based on genetic and morphological characteristics. Lerchia baccata's classification is subject to ongoing research and refinement within the field of plant taxonomy.

The plant's extracts have been traditionally used to treat various health issues, such as digestive problems and skin conditions. Its bark and leaves have been used in folk medicine to reduce fever and alleviate respiratory issues. The plant's berries, although toxic in large quantities, have been used in some traditional remedies for their supposed antiviral properties.
